mnemonics: U.U.I. (User Understands It) - Good UX means the user intuitively understands the interface
The 4 C's of Design Perspectives: Conceptual, Concrete, Critical, Creative
PERSPECTIVE: People Expect Really Smooth, Predictable, Engaging, Clear, Thoughtful, Intuitive, Valuable Experiences
keyPoints: Design perspectives include conceptual (idea), concrete (implementation), critical (evaluation), and creative (innovation) viewpoints
Good UI/UX balances business goals with user needs and technical constraints
User-centered design requires empathy and understanding of user mental models
commonMistakes: Designing for yourself instead of the target user
Prioritizing aesthetics over functionality and usability
Skipping user testing and validation steps
